<insights>

- High cohesion (0.83) — files are tightly interconnected
- The gateway modules (rex-gateway.ts, domain-gateway.ts) are co-located with usage analytics services — gateways are architectural infrastructure shared across the entire server layer, so co-locating them with a specific analytics feature can obscure their broader role
- Cohesion of 0.83 is strong, suggesting usage tracking, scheduling, and gateway modules genuinely cluster together in the import graph — the scheduler likely wires usage cleanup to the gateway-supplied rex data
- Unit tests for all three service files (incremental-task-usage, task-usage, usage-cleanup-scheduler) are present in this zone — good test coverage discipline for this subsystem
- Gateway modules (rex-gateway.ts, domain-gateway.ts) are grouped into the analytics zone by the community detector — consider whether they should be treated as a separate 'server infrastructure' zone to make the gateway boundary more visible in tooling.
- All service files in this zone have corresponding unit tests co-located in the same community cluster, indicating well-maintained test discipline for the usage analytics subsystem.
- sourcevision and rex-unit each import 1 file from this zone (per cross-zone imports) — verify these are importing through the gateway modules rather than directly from analytics services, to preserve the gateway pattern.
- [call graph] 114 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 66 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
